rationale: A confirmed exploit chain is evidenced: Apache ActiveMQ (CVE-2023-46604 or similar RCE) spawned cmd.exe (A0, EV-001) which used certutil to download a randomly-named executable from an internal attacker-controlled host (10.0.2.13:8080) and immediately executed it (EV-003, EV-004, EV-005, EV-006). The dropped payload (qSwUwejx.exe, original_file_name '-', indicating stripped PE metadata) then established a TCP connection to 10.0.2.13:4444 — a classic reverse-shell port (EV-007). The payload subsequently spawned an interactive cmd.exe shell (EV-008, EV-009), confirming active post-exploitation, and then used certutil again to download AnyDesk from the internet (EV-011, EV-012), a well-known persistence/remote-access technique. The full kill chain — RCE via Java/ActiveMQ → download-and-execute → reverse shell → C2 callback → AnyDesk installation — is directly evidenced with no plausible benign explanation.
